behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in real CLI-to-git interaction and externally visible stdout contract: rename detection from an actual repo must map to one category, not split across add/delete. Unit tests on classification logic alone can miss subprocess/output-shape issues. Not Agentic/Workflow because behavior text contains no AI/ML call or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI, and assert stdout report shows the file only under renamed with no corresponding added/deleted entries.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly needs real git diff behavior for binary content plus CLI process stability/exit behavior and stdout counting. In-process fakes can hide real binary diff parsing/subprocess edge cases. Not Agentic/Workflow: no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI against that change set, assert successful process completion and report includes binary file counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The no-commit repo state is a real git external condition (unborn HEAD) that often surfaces via subprocess error paths; correctness is the CLI’s externally visible behavior (empty report, no error). Unit-only tests won’t reliably expose this contract. Not Agentic/Workflow by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a brand-new git repo with zero commits, run the CLI, and assert exit is non-error and stdout is a valid empty report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim against a real repository history and the CLI’s external report output; it requires round-trip observation of externally visible results from real repo state. Integration tests with mocked git history can give false confidence. No AI/ML dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run the CLI, and compare reported totals/categories to independently computed expected counts from that history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics depend on real git index/worktree behavior plus CLI output contract; mocks often miss index-state edge cases. This is an external CLI equivalence check, not just a local function assertion. No model judgment involved, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run the CLI for each state, and assert identical structured stdout output.
